heading: International food security technical assistance
label: 1736dd International food security technical assistance
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990, and not as part of the Food for Peace Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Definition of international food security
children.label: a Definition of international food security
children.text: In this section, the term “international food security” means access by any person at any time to food and nutrition that is sufficient for a healthy and productive life.

children.heading: Collection of information
children.label: b Collection of information
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) shall compile information from appropriate mission areas of the Department of Agriculture (including the Food, Nutrition, and Consumer Services mission area) relating to the improvement of international food security.

children.label: A
children.text: a Federal, State, or local agency;

children.label: B
children.text: an agency or instrumentality of the government of a foreign country;

children.label: C
children.text: a domestic or international organization, including a domestic or international nongovernmental organization; and

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: d Technical assistance
children.text: On request by an entity described in subsection (c)(2), the Secretary may provide technical assistance to the entity to implement a program for the improvement of international food security.

children.heading: Program priority
children.label: e Program priority
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall give priority to programs relating to the development of food and nutrition safety net systems with a focus on food insecure countries.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
